topic: Consolidate the full data-quality program from all prior lineages into one shippable, safe GOVERNANCE and ROLLOUT layer: unified rollout sequence, corpus migration plan, safety/governance model, measurement plan, consolidated NO-GO + anti-patterns

novelty: The five inviolable dependency edges (census-before-gate, engine-before-front-doors, backfill-before-error, coverage-guard-before-retrieval-trust, C2-before-retrieval-promotion) topologically sort into ONE 17-stage / 7-phase master sequence merging dq-automation-impl's 11 stages + the novel slate + the per-surface detectors; the novel correctness/adherence half (Phase VI) parallelizes with the floor-bypassing half, the retrieval half (Phase VII) is strictly last
ruledOut: interleaving retrieval stages with floor-bypassing stages (breaks the C2-before-promotion edge)
forcing the report-only novel slate behind the migration (it has no migration dependency)

focus: KQ2 migration plan for the existing corpus
novelty: The migration is a four-beat per-gate discipline (WARN -> BACKFILL -> RE-MEASURE-TO-ZERO -> ERROR) that does not compress, built on the Stage-0 census; the doc-gate migration (ships on cost) and the retrieval migration (frozen behind the net-new coverage guard + dual-cache-key fix) are TWO migrations with different gates; the legacy-corpus invariant is no-error-before-backfill-zero
ruledOut: compressing warn->error into a single flip (the premature-error trap; census exists because the band is asserted-not-counted)
treating the retrieval re-index as part of the doc-gate migration (disjoint gates)

novelty: Four components: the fixClass taxonomy (safe/guarded/report-only) frozen in detector-registry.ts unifying EVERY detector under two structural invariants (INV-1 no body auto-fix, INV-2 no retrieval promotion without prod@3); per-stage rollback named before each stage runs; four human-in-the-loop boundaries (auto-safe-fix / --confirm-guarded / always-human-authored body / release-reviewer prod@3); four standing drift guards (embedding/coverage/storage/cross-copy)
ruledOut: per-detector ad-hoc safe/risky flags (classification is a property of the FIX; one frozen registry)
--confirm unlocking risky/body-touching fixes (gates the safe tier only)

focus: KQ4 measurement plan (how each tier earns its keep)
novelty: One-reader-one-metric-no-cross-credit is the governing principle. Four tiers each with a named metric + proof obligation + earns-its-keep bar: write-time = conformance-to-zero + scratch-packet regression-catch; scheduled = escape-class defects (path-filter/backfill-blind-spot/cross-surface) the change-triggered tiers structurally miss; retrieval = prod-mode completeRecall@3 RISE ONLY (external @5/@10/@20 and eval@K inadmissible); novel = each on its own non-retrieval metric. The scheduled tier's falsification condition is novel to this synthesis
ruledOut: promoting a retrieval candidate on external @5/@10/@20 or eval@K (the floor hides that band)
crediting the write-time quality-loop score as a retrieval improvement (a high form score is not a retrieval win)

novelty: 18 merged de-duplicated NO-GOs each with a deciding reason, derived from 10 generative governance anti-patterns (eval-mode saturation, silent-drop, destructive auto-fix, net-negative rollup, mixed-vector confound, rebuild-shipped-machinery, five-engines, premature-error, change-triggered blind spot, proxy-as-truth); the concrete list is a derived consequence and a future candidate is judged by which anti-pattern it trips
ruledOut: treating the NO-GO list as a static blocklist (it is derived from the 10 anti-patterns)
re-litigating the cross-lineage corrections (items 7,16 are settled)

focus: KQ6 cross-lineage reconciliation into one governance spine
novelty: All three cross-lineage conflicts share ONE shape (build-vs-wire) and the Stage-0 census is their common resolver: extend-quality-loop vs don't (quarantine the destructive loop, reuse the pure scorer); no-retroactive-automation vs 8-CI-workflows (only the cron tier is empty); build-wikilink-validator vs already-CI-wired (add only the missing timing). The spine's first rule = confirm what ships before authoring anything
ruledOut: carrying both sides of a conflict to be safe (an incoherent program)
treating the conflicts as open questions (settled by file:line; reconciliation is selection)

focus: KQ7 adversarial: governance gaps, sequencing contradictions, convergence
novelty: Converged. Six attacks: novel-slate parallelism SURVIVES (entity catalog/causal graph independent of doc gates); S9-drift-before-S14-C2 is a LOAD-BEARING edge not a preference; CI-report-only holds (boundary is blast-radius-human-reviewed not fix-safety); ONE real gap closed (the fixClass registry guards itself with INV-1/INV-2, a guarded-class change); S5-capability-before-S7-use is non-contradictory; RAIL-2 has NO escape path (every retrieval item routes through C2, the assembler correctly bypasses as non-retrieval). Zero deliverables overturned
ruledOut: a faster path shipping retrieval before C2 (RAIL-2 forecloses it)
letting CI auto-commit safe fixes (blast-radius-not-fix-safety boundary)
